home.hero_subtitle: Reliable agricultural knowledge and insights — from government schemes and weather forecasts to crop guidance and market prices.
home.weather_desc: Weather is the single biggest factor affecting crop success. Our Weather Advisory tool gives you real-time conditions — temperature, rainfall probability, and wind speed — so you can decide the right day to sow, spray, or irrigate.
home.weather_why1_desc: Know the right window to plant before rains arrive or dry spells hit.
home.weather_why2_desc: Avoid waste — only spray pesticides when wind and humidity are ideal.
home.weather_why3_desc: Skip watering when rain is forecast. Save water, save cost.
home.stats_subtitle: Accurate agricultural data to help farmers make well-informed decisions every season.
home.stats_c1_desc: Explore production volumes across crops and regions with clear, accessible breakdowns for every farmer.
home.stats_c2_desc: Understand nutrient levels and soil conditions with practical guidance for healthier, more productive fields.
home.stats_c3_desc: Learn from seasonal yield patterns across regions to better plan your next crop cycle.
home.stats_c4_desc: Stay informed on historical and current crop prices so you can choose the best time to sell.
home.challenges_subtitle: Agriculture today is more complex than ever. Farmers deserve access to the right knowledge at the right time.
home.ch1_desc: Shifting climate patterns demand timely weather forecasts and adaptive crop planning to protect livelihoods.
home.ch2_desc: Soil degradation and pest resistance call for updated farming techniques and research-backed crop guidance.
home.ch3_desc: Unpredictable price swings make access to current market information essential for confident selling decisions.
home.ch4_desc: Many farmers miss out on government subsidies and welfare programs simply due to lack of accessible information.
home.modern_subtitle: Bringing farmers closer to trusted knowledge — from university research and KVK advisories to weather data and sustainable farming practices.

home.card1_desc: Access weather updates, market prices, plant protection tips, and advisory services for farmers.
home.card2_desc: Online trading platform for agricultural commodities connecting farmers to buyers across India.
home.card3_desc: Insurance scheme providing financial protection to farmers against crop loss due to natural calamities.

home.g1_desc: Research-backed crop practices and seasonal farming guides from India's leading agricultural institutions.
home.g2_desc: Official advisories, scheme announcements, and policy updates directly from central and state authorities.

home.s1_desc: Explore ongoing agriculture development programs and farmer welfare initiatives from across India.
home.s2_desc: Discover financial assistance available for seeds, equipment, fertilizers, and modern farming tools.

about.hero_subtitle: A platform built to give farmers direct access to the knowledge, tools, and information they need — without intermediaries.
about.who_content: Cropyaan is a digital platform designed for Indian farmers. It brings together crop guidance, weather advisories, government scheme information, and market data into a single, accessible place. We are not an agricultural company. We do not sell products or inputs. We provide structured, reliable information to help farmers make informed decisions about their land and livelihood.
about.mission_content: Farming decisions are time-sensitive and consequential. Yet access to accurate, actionable information has historically been uneven — concentrated in extension services, research institutions, or urban centres that many farmers cannot easily reach. Cropyaan exists to close that gap. Our mission is to make agricultural knowledge freely and simply available to every farmer, regardless of location or digital literacy.
about.vision_content: We are building toward a future where no farming decision is made in an information vacuum. Our immediate goals are to expand language coverage across Indian regional languages, deepen the quality and locality of weather and soil data, and make government scheme information easier to understand and act on. Over time, we intend for Cropyaan to become the most trusted source of practical agricultural information in India.
about.closing: Cropyaan is built with farmers, for farmers. We measure our progress by how useful we are to the people working the land.

hiw.hero_subtitle: Discover how drones are transforming farming with smarter, faster, and more precise insights
hiw.step1_desc: Farming drones can reduce pesticide and fertilizer usage by up to 30–50% by targeting only affected areas instead of spraying entire fields. This not only lowers input costs for farmers but also minimizes environmental impact, reducing soil degradation and chemical runoff into water sources.
hiw.step2_desc: A single agricultural drone can survey 20–40 acres in under 30 minutes, saving hours compared to manual inspection. This speed allows farmers to monitor fields more frequently, identify issues early, and respond quickly before problems spread across large areas.
hiw.step3_title: Boosts Crop Yield with Data Insights
hiw.step3_desc: Drones equipped with multispectral cameras can detect crop stress early, helping farmers improve yield by up to 15–20%. By analyzing plant health, moisture levels, and disease patterns, farmers can take precise actions instead of relying on guesswork.
hiw.step4_desc: Drones can easily operate in hilly regions, flooded fields, or muddy soil, where traditional machinery struggles. This makes them especially valuable in regions with challenging geography, ensuring no part of the farm goes unmonitored or untreated.
hiw.step5_desc: With government support like subsidies and initiatives, agricultural drone adoption in India is rapidly increasing. As costs decrease and awareness grows, more farmers are turning to drones for spraying, mapping, and improving overall farm productivity.
hiw.closing_subtitle: Every resource on CropYaan is chosen to reduce uncertainty and give farmers the confidence to plan smarter — across every season, every crop, and every region.

weather.hero_subtitle: Enter your location to get current weather conditions and tailored farming advice for your fields.
weather.placeholder: Enter city or district name (e.g. Pune, Warangal, Nashik)
weather.error_not_found: Location not found. Please try a different city name.
weather.error_fetch: Unable to fetch weather data. Please check your connection and try again.
weather.footer_note: Weather data sourced from Open-Meteo. Advisories are general guidance — consult your local KVK or agricultural officer for crop-specific recommendations.
weather.adv_rain_text: Rainfall likely. Consider delaying irrigation and avoid applying fertilizers that may wash away.
weather.adv_heat_text: Extreme heat detected. Ensure crops receive adequate water and consider shade netting for sensitive plants.
weather.adv_wind_text: High wind speeds expected. Avoid pesticide and herbicide spraying — drift can damage crops.
weather.adv_cold_text: Cold conditions ahead. Protect frost-sensitive crops and delay sowing of warm-season varieties.
weather.adv_good_text: Weather looks good for farming activities today. Suitable for sowing, spraying, and field maintenance.
weather.summary_none: Weather conditions are broadly favorable. No significant risks detected for field operations today.
weather.risk_heat_stress_reason: Temperature is above 32°C. Crops face accelerating evapotranspiration and cellular heat damage. Soil surface can reach 50°C+, scorching shallow roots.
weather.risk_cold_stress_reason: Temperature below 12°C slows root metabolism and nutrient uptake. Below 4°C, ice crystal formation damages plant cell walls. Phosphorus and nitrogen absorption drops sharply.
weather.risk_excess_rain_reason: High rain probability indicates likely precipitation. Saturated soil reduces oxygen in the root zone, increasing waterlogging risk and causing runoff that strips applied fertilizers.
weather.risk_drought_risk_reason: Low rain probability combined with high temperature creates rapid soil moisture depletion. Sandy or loamy soils may become hydrophobic within 48–72 hours without irrigation.
weather.risk_wind_damage_reason: High wind increases plant transpiration, causing water stress even when soil moisture is adequate. Above 40 km/h, stem lodging and damage to flowering structures can occur.
weather.risk_fungal_pressure_reason: Warm and wet conditions create ideal conditions for fungal pathogens. Leaf blight, powdery mildew, and root rot thrive when the canopy stays moist for 6+ hours.
weather.risk_spray_risk_reason: High wind or rain probability makes pesticide and foliar feed applications ineffective. Spray drift can travel 20m off-target and rain will wash off product before absorption.
weather.risk_excess_rain_reason_short: High rain probability causing waterlogging risk.
weather.risk_drought_risk_reason_short: Low rain and high heat depleting soil moisture.
weather.risk_wind_damage_reason_short: High winds increasing plant water stress.
weather.risk_fungal_pressure_reason_short: Warm wet conditions favoring fungal growth.
weather.risk_spray_risk_reason_short: Wind or rain making spray applications ineffective.
weather.action_heat_1: Irrigate fields in the early morning (before 8 AM) to minimize evaporation loss
weather.action_heat_2: Avoid all field operations between 11 AM – 4 PM
weather.action_heat_3: Apply mulch to retain soil moisture and reduce surface temperature
weather.action_heat_4: Monitor for wilting symptoms — especially in young seedlings
weather.action_cold_1: Apply mulching around root zones to insulate against ground frost
weather.action_cold_2: Delay fertilizer application — nutrients will not be absorbed in cold soil
weather.action_cold_3: Avoid sowing new seeds until temperatures recover above 14°C
weather.action_rain_1: Clear drainage channels before rainfall arrives to prevent waterlogging
weather.action_rain_2: Hold off on fertilizer application — high washoff risk
weather.action_rain_3: Avoid heavy machinery on fields to prevent soil compaction under wet conditions
weather.action_drought_1: Begin supplemental irrigation — check soil moisture at 10 cm depth
weather.action_drought_2: Prioritize irrigation for crops at flowering or grain-fill stage
weather.action_drought_3: Consider drip irrigation to maximise efficiency under dry conditions
weather.action_wind_1: Stake tall or flowering plants to prevent lodging
weather.action_wind_2: Postpone all aerial and spray operations until wind drops below 20 km/h
weather.action_wind_3: Inspect for snapped stems or uprooted seedlings after wind event
weather.action_fungal_1: Scout fields for early fungal symptoms — act before visible spread
weather.action_fungal_2: Apply preventive fungicide if rain is expected to persist beyond 2 days
weather.action_fungal_3: Improve canopy airflow where possible — avoid dense over-irrigation
weather.action_spray_1: Postpone pesticide and foliar fertilizer applications to a calm, dry window
weather.action_spray_2: If spraying is unavoidable, use low-drift nozzles and apply early morning

services.hero_subtitle: From daily weather to crop-specific advice, CropYaan puts the right information in front of you at the right time.
services.weather_desc: Farming decisions are time-sensitive. Our weather advisory gives you hyper-local forecasts so you can plan irrigation, sowing, and harvesting without second-guessing the sky.
services.weather_b1: Daily and weekly forecasts tailored to your district
services.weather_b2: Rain probability and wind alerts before critical farm operations
services.weather_b3: Smart irrigation scheduling based on live weather data
services.weather_b4: Early warnings for frost, heat stress, and unseasonal rain
services.crop_desc: Not every crop needs the same care in the same week. CropYaan delivers advice that matches your specific crop, your growth stage, and the conditions on your land right now.
services.crop_b1: Stage-by-stage guidance from sowing to harvest
services.crop_b2: Pest and disease alerts before they spread
services.crop_b3: Fertiliser and water recommendations by crop type
services.crop_b4: Market timing suggestions to reduce post-harvest losses
services.insights_desc: We are building a layer of insights that connects government crop data, market trends, and regional performance — so you have more than gut instinct to go on.
services.insights_b1: Crop price trends from mandis across the country
services.insights_b2: Government MSP updates and procurement announcements
services.insights_b3: Regional yield benchmarks to compare your performance
services.insights_b4: Soil health data overlaid with seasonal recommendations
services.chatbot_desc: Our AI assistant is built specifically for farming queries — plain language in, practical advice out.
services.chatbot_b1: Ask about pests, soil, weather, or schemes in any language
services.chatbot_b2: Responses grounded in agronomic knowledge, not generic search
services.chatbot_b3: Remembers your crop and location for personalised replies
services.chatbot_b4: Available anytime — no wait, no call centres
services.cta_subtitle: CropYaan is growing alongside the farmers who use it. More features, better data, and sharper advice — every month.

rice.name: Rice
rice.description: Staple food crop requiring high water availability and warm humid climate.
rice.tips: Requires standing water during early growth. Transplanting gives better yield than direct sowing.

wheat.name: Wheat
wheat.description: Cool-season crop suited to well-drained, fertile plains.
wheat.tips: Sow after monsoon. Needs 4–5 irrigations. Harvest before summer heat.

maize.name: Maize
maize.description: Versatile warm-season crop for food, fodder, and industrial use.
maize.tips: Well-drained soils are critical. Avoid waterlogging. Intercrop with legumes for soil health.

cotton.name: Cotton
cotton.description: Major fiber crop thriving in deep black soils with warm dry climate.
cotton.tips: Deep black soil retains moisture well. Avoid excessive rain during boll formation.

soybean.name: Soybean
soybean.description: Protein-rich legume that fixes nitrogen, improving soil for next crops.
soybean.tips: Short-duration variety recommended for late sowings. Inoculate seeds with Rhizobium.

groundnut.name: Groundnut
groundnut.description: Drought-tolerant oilseed crop well-adapted to light sandy soils.
groundnut.tips: Sandy loam soil ensures easy pod penetration and harvest. Calcium is critical for pod fill.

sugarcane.name: Sugarcane
sugarcane.description: Long-duration cash crop needing fertile soil and ample water.
sugarcane.tips: Ratoon cropping saves cost after primary harvest. Drip irrigation increases water efficiency.

bajra.name: Pearl Millet (Bajra)
bajra.description: Highly drought-resistant millet for arid and semi-arid zones.
bajra.tips: Grows well in poor, sandy soils. Extremely heat and drought tolerant. Sow at monsoon onset.

jowar.name: Sorghum (Jowar)
jowar.description: Multi-purpose cereal for food, fodder, and ethanol production.
jowar.tips: Rabi Jowar is more nutritious than Kharif. Avoid waterlogging. Tolerates moderate drought.

mustard.name: Mustard
mustard.description: Major rabi oilseed crop grown across the northern plains.
mustard.tips: Cool climate at sowing stage and warm dry weather at maturity gives best oil content.

potato.name: Potato
potato.description: High-value vegetable crop requiring cool climate and well-drained soil.
potato.tips: Loose, well-drained soil ensures tuber development. Hill up after 30 days to prevent greening.

sunflower.name: Sunflower
sunflower.description: Day-length neutral oilseed crop suitable across all three seasons.
sunflower.tips: Highly photo-insensitive — can be grown year-round. Needs adequate boron for seed set.

tomato.name: Tomato
tomato.description: High-value vegetable crop for zaid and rabi seasons in warm regions.
tomato.tips: Stake plants to prevent disease. Drip irrigation reduces foliar disease significantly.

tea.name: Tea
tea.description: Perennial plantation crop for humid, hilly terrain with acidic laterite soils.
tea.tips: Acidic soil (pH 4.5–5.5) is essential. Shade trees help maintain humidity. Regular pruning for bushes.

coconut.name: Coconut
coconut.description: Tropical palm crop for coastal and humid inland areas.
coconut.tips: Saline coastal soils suit coconut well. Intercrop with banana or cacao for added income.

crop_engine.warn_soil: Soil type may not be ideal
crop_engine.advisory_extreme_heat: Extreme heat — use heat-tolerant varieties and ensure irrigation.
crop_engine.advisory_extreme_cold: Very cold temperatures — protect crops from frost damage.
crop_engine.advisory_low_monsoon: Very low monsoon rainfall — heavy irrigation dependency.
crop_engine.advisory_excess_rain: Excess rainfall detected — prioritize drainage and flood-tolerant crops.
